/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-tom-ANDES-cameo/9e0bd2d8-e804-49c4-9209-7e6d629adac1/scratchpad/drs-flowdown.html
<title>ANDES DRS requirement flow-down</title>
<meta name="description" content="The 14 requirements linked to the SW DRS work package, traced through their derivation chains to the source documents.">
<style>
  :root {
    --paper: #f4f5f1;
    --card: #ffffff;
    --ink: #20262c;
    --muted: #626b74;
    --line: #d8dcd3;
    --edge: #97a0a8;
    --trs: #2e5fa3;
    --ins: #a06722;
    --wp-bg: #232e33;
    --wp-text: #e8ece6;
  }
  html { background: var(--paper); }
  body {
    font-family: "Avenir Next", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif;
    color: var(--ink);
    margin: 0;
    line-height: 1.45;
  }
  .mono { font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace; }

  header { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 44px 28px 8px; }
  .eyebrow {
    font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace;
    font-size: 11px;
    letter-spacing: 0.14em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: var(--muted);
    margin: 0 0 10px;
  }
  h1 {
    font-size: 30px;
    font-weight: 600;
    letter-spacing: -0.01em;
    margin: 0 0 10px;
    text-wrap: balance;
  }
  .sub { max-width: 62ch; color: var(--muted); font-size: 15px; margin: 0 0 20px; }
  .sub strong { color: var(--ink); font-weight: 600; }

  .stats { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 6px; }
  .stat {
    background: var(--card);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 3px;
    padding: 7px 14px;
    font-size: 13px;
    color: var(--muted);
  }
  .stat b {
    color: var(--ink);
    font-weight: 600;
    font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
    margin-right: 2px;
  }

  .legend {
    max-width: 1200px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    padding: 10px 28px 18px;
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 8px 26px;
    font-size: 12.5px;
    color: var(--muted);
    align-items: center;
  }
  .key { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; }
  .swatch { width: 11px; height: 11px; border-radius: 2px; display: inline-block; }
  .ln { width: 34px; height: 0; border-top: 2px solid var(--edge); display: inline-block; }
  .ln.dotted { border-top-style: dotted; }
  .ln.dashed { border-top-style: dashed; }

  .scroll { overflow-x: auto; padding: 0 12px 30px; }
  .grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 1fr 40px 1fr 40px 1fr 40px 158px;
    grid-auto-flow: row;
    min-width: 1060px;
    max-width: 1240px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    padding: 0 16px;
  }

  .lvl {
    font-size: 10.5px;
    letter-spacing: 0.13em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: var(--muted);
    padding: 6px 2px 10px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line);
    align-self: end;
  }
  .lvl.c1 { grid-column: 1; }
  .lvl.c3 { grid-column: 3; }
  .lvl.c5 { grid-column: 5; }
  .lvl.c7 { grid-column: 7; grid-row: 1; padding-left: 14px; }

  .ghead {
    grid-column: 1 / 7;
    font-size: 12px;
    font-weight: 600;
    letter-spacing: 0.08em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: var(--ink);
    padding: 26px 0 10px;
  }

  .card {
    background: var(--card);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 3px;
    padding: 10px 13px 11px;
    margin: 7px 0;
    font-size: 12.5px;
    align-self: center;
    width: 100%;
    box-sizing: border-box;
  }
  .card p { margin: 5px 0 0; color: #3a424b; }
  .card .rid {
    font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace;
    font-size: 12px;
    font-weight: 700;
    font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  }
  .card.trs .rid { color: var(--trs); }
  .card.ins .rid { color: var(--ins); }
  .card .note {
    display: block;
    margin-top: 6px;
    font-size: 11px;
    color: var(--muted);
    font-style: italic;
  }
  .span3 { grid-row: span 3; }

  /* connectors: horizontal line + arrowhead, drawn at the cell's vertical center */
  .conn { position: relative; }
  .conn::before {
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    left: 0; right: 5px; top: 50%;
    border-top: 2px solid var(--edge);
  }
  .conn::after {
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    right: 0;
    top: 50%;
    margin-top: -4.5px;
    border-left: 7px solid var(--edge);
    border-top: 4.5px solid transparent;
    border-bottom: 4.5px solid transparent;
  }
  .conn.dashed::before { border-top-style: dashed; }
  .conn em {
    position: absolute;
    top: 50%;
    left: 50%;
    transform: translate(-50%, -190%);
    font-style: normal;
    font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace;
    font-size: 10px;
    color: var(--muted);
    white-space: nowrap;
    background: var(--paper);
    padding: 0 4px;
  }
  .conn.direct { grid-column: 2 / 7; }

  /* branch: trunk + per-row horizontal segments for the R-INS-896 subtree */
  .conn.branch::before { left: 50%; border-top-style: dotted; }
  .conn.branch::after { display: none; }
  .conn.branch .ah {
    position: absolute;
    right: 0; top: 50%;
    margin-top: -4.5px;
    border-left: 7px solid var(--edge);
    border-top: 4.5px solid transparent;
    border-bottom: 4.5px solid transparent;
  }
  .conn.branch .v {
    position: absolute;
    left: calc(50% - 1px);
    border-left: 2px dotted var(--edge);
  }
  .branch.btop .v { top: 50%; bottom: 0; }
  .branch.bmid .v { top: 0; bottom: 0; }
  .branch.bbot .v { top: 0; height: 50%; }
  .branch.bmid { --in: 1; }
  .branch.bmid::after {
    display: block;
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    left: 0; right: 50%; top: 50%;
    border-top: 2px dotted var(--edge);
    border-left: none; border-bottom: none;
    margin: 0;
  }

  .wp {
    grid-column: 7;
    grid-row: 2 / 15;
    background: var(--wp-bg);
    color: var(--wp-text);
    border-radius: 3px;
    margin: 7px 0 7px 0;
    padding: 16px 15px;
  }
  .wp .inner { position: sticky; top: 34vh; }
  .wp .wbs {
    font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace;
    font-size: 10px;
    letter-spacing: 0.1em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: #93a49b;
    margin: 0 0 8px;
  }
  .wp h2 { font-size: 17px; font-weight: 600; margin: 0 0 6px; }
  .wp p { font-size: 12px; color: #b8c2ba; margin: 0 0 10px; }
  .wp .count {
    font-size: 12px;
    color: var(--wp-text);
    border-top: 1px solid #3d4a47;
    padding-top: 10px;
    font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  }

  footer {
    max-width: 1200px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    padding: 6px 28px 48px;
    font-size: 12.5px;
    color: var(--muted);
    max-width: 78ch;
  }
  footer p { margin: 0 0 8px; }
  footer .mono { font-size: 11.5px; }
</style>

<header>
  <p class="eyebrow">ANDES &middot; extracted from ANDES_model.mdzip (Cameo / MagicDraw XMI)</p>
  <h1>Requirements on the Data Reduction Software work package</h1>
  <p class="sub">All <strong>14 requirements</strong> linked to the <strong>SW DRS</strong> work package
  in the model's WBS, traced upward through their derivation chains to the two source documents
  they flow down from. Reading direction: document requirement &rarr; SW-level derivation &rarr;
  DRS-level requirement &rarr; work package.</p>
  <div class="stats">
    <div class="stat"><b>14</b> linked requirements</div>
    <div class="stat"><b>10</b> reached via flow-down chains</div>
    <div class="stat"><b>4</b> apply directly from the document</div>
    <div class="stat"><b>2</b> source documents</div>
  </div>
</header>

<div class="legend">
  <span class="key"><span class="swatch" style="background:var(--trs)"></span>ANDES Technical Requirements Specification</span>
  <span class="key"><span class="swatch" style="background:var(--ins)"></span>Common Requirements for ELT Instruments</span>
  <span class="key"><span class="ln"></span>DeriveReqt</span>
  <span class="key"><span class="ln dotted"></span>containment</span>
  <span class="key"><span class="ln dashed"></span>implied by naming</span>
</div>

<div class="scroll">
<div class="grid">
<!--ROWS-->
<div class="wp">
  <div class="inner">
    <p class="wbs">6.WBS / ANDES_WP / SW Sys Eng</p>
    <h2>SW DRS</h2>
    <p>Data Reduction Software work package of the ANDES SW subsystem</p>
    <div class="count">14 requirements linked</div>
  </div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<footer>
  <p><strong>How this was derived.</strong> Requirement&ndash;work-package links are the model's
  dependencies whose client is the <span class="mono">SW DRS</span> class in
  <span class="mono">6.WBS</span>. Upward chains follow <span class="mono">DeriveReqt</span>
  abstractions; for the R-INS-896 flow-down the model nests the SW1&ndash;SW3 requirements inside
  their parent (shown dotted). One link, <span class="mono">[R-AND-88].1 &rarr; [R-AND-88].1.SW1</span>,
  has no modeled relationship and is implied by the naming convention only (shown dashed).</p>
  <p>[R-AND-112] additionally flows down to the SCAO work packages; only the DRS branch is shown here.
  Requirement texts are quoted verbatim from the model.</p>
</footer>

Tool reply2026-07-04T09:45:26.914Z

Assistant2026-07-04T09:45:41.679Z

Done — the flow-down visualization is live: https://claude.ai/code/artifact/59108c49-d09b-426a-bd3c-2eb304bf23f6

What I found

The SW DRS work package (in 6.WBS / ANDES_WP / SW Sys Eng) has 14 requirements linked to it, coming from two source documents:

ANDES Technical Requirements Specification (8 requirements) - Seven flow-down chains, each three levels deep: top-level instrument requirement → SW-level derivation → DRS-level requirement. For example: [R-AND-65] "two observing modes" → [R-AND-65].3 "SW shall support both modes" → [R-AND-65].3.SW2 "DRS shall reduce seeing-limited and IFU data". The chains cover observing modes, wavelength range extension (U/K band), spectral resolution preservation (±5%), calibration validity (no night-time calibrations), sky subtraction (<1%), interpolation (FWHM growth <20%), and ELT data-flow compliance. - [R-AND-120] (the DRS section of the TRS) applies directly with no intermediate derivation.

Common Requirements for ELT Instruments (6 requirements) - [R-INS-896] (data reduction package) flows down to .1 and branches into three DRS leaves: quality-control monitoring in the reduction cascade, interactive science-grade reduction, and CPL/HDRL/Reflex compliance. - [R-INS-928], [R-INS-1049], [R-INS-1050] (Phase 3 / Science Data Product standards) apply directly.

Two honest caveats, shown in the diagram

  • One link, [R-AND-88].1 → [R-AND-88].1.SW1, has no modeled DeriveReqt — I inferred it from the naming convention only (drawn dashed, labeled "implied"). That may be a gap worth fixing in the model.
  • The [R-INS-896] flow-downs aren't linked by DeriveReqt either; the model expresses derivation there by nesting the child requirements inside the parent (drawn dotted).

All requirement texts in the diagram are quoted verbatim from the XMI. The extraction scripts (index_model.py, trace_flowdown.py) are in the scratchpad, so we can rerun this for any other work package — the same WP-dependency pattern is used throughout the WBS.

Assistant2026-07-04T09:55:57.910Z

Good question — let me ground my answer in the model itself before opining. Two quick checks: does the TRS contain radial-velocity/precision requirements that don't reach the DRS work package, and do the 14 linked requirements have any verification links?

Assistant2026-07-04T09:56:12.702Z
📝 Write gap_check.py
/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-tom-ANDES-cameo/9e0bd2d8-e804-49c4-9209-7e6d629adac1/scratchpad/gap_check.py
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# dependencies = []
# ///
"""Check: (1) RV/wavelength-cal requirements and where they flow;
(2) verification links on the 14 DRS WP requirements."""
import json
import pickle
import re

with open("model_index.pkl", "rb") as f:
    ix = pickle.load(f)
E, D, R, S = ix["elements"], ix["deps"], ix["requirements"], ix["stereo_of_dep"]

def doc_of(eid):
    parts = []
    while eid:
        e = E.get(eid)
        if not e:
            break
        parts.append(e["name"])
        eid = e["parent"]
    for i, p in enumerate(parts):
        if p == "0.Requirements" and i >= 1:
            return parts[i - 1]
    return parts[-2] if len(parts) >= 2 else ""

by_client, by_supplier = {}, {}
for did, d in D.items():
    for c in d["clients"]:
        by_client.setdefault(c, []).append(did)
    for s in d["suppliers"]:
        by_supplier.setdefault(s, []).append(did)

WP = "_2022x_1_ef20353_1686237423990_559313_159"

print("### RV / wavelength-calibration requirements in ANDES TRS:")
pat = re.compile(r"(radial velocity|\bRV\b|cm/s|m/s|wavelength calibration|wavelength accuracy)", re.I)
hits = []
for base, r in R.items():
    if not r["text"] or not pat.search(r["text"]):
        continue
    name = E.get(base, {}).get("name", "")
    doc = doc_of(base)
    if "ANDES Technical" not in doc:
        continue
    hits.append((name, base, r["text"]))
hits.sort()
for name, base, text in hits:
    # where does it flow (as supplier or client of DeriveReqt / plain deps)?
    targets = set()
    for did in by_client.get(base, []) + by_supplier.get(base, []):
        d = D[did]
        for o in d["clients"] + d["suppliers"]:
            if o != base:
                n = E.get(o, {}).get("name", "")
                if n:
                    targets.add(n)
    t = ", ".join(sorted(targets)[:8]) or "NO LINKS"
    print(f"  {name}: -> {t}")
    print(f"     {text[:150]}")

print("\n### Verification / satisfy links on the 14 WP requirements:")
with open("flowdown.json") as f:
    fd = json.load(f)
wp_reqs = fd["wp_reqs"]
for eid in wp_reqs:
    name = E.get(eid, {}).get("name", "")
    vlinks = []
    for did in by_client.get(eid, []) + by_supplier.get(eid, []):
        stereos = S.get(did, ["plain"])
        for st in stereos:
            if st in ("Verify", "Satisfy", "Refine", "Justify"):
                d = D[did]
                others = [E.get(o, {}).get("name", o) for o in d["clients"] + d["suppliers"] if o != eid]
                vlinks.append(f"{st} <-> {others}")
    print(f"  {name}: {vlinks if vlinks else 'none'}")

print("\n### 'Reduction Pipeline RV Error' element:")
for eid, e in E.items():
    if "RV Error" in (e["name"] or ""):
        parts = []
        p = eid
        while p:
            parts.append(E[p]["name"])
            p = E[p]["parent"]
        print(f"  {e['name']} ({e['type']})  path: {' < '.join(parts[1:5])}")

Check RV requirement flow and verification links

cd "$SCRATCH" && uv run gap_check.py 2>&1 | head -100
Tool reply2026-07-04T09:56:22.322Z
### RV / wavelength-calibration requirements in ANDES TRS:
  AO WFS camera lens stage: -> AO_Software, Correction of M4-act Vs WFS.SA misalignement, WFS_Electronic_design, WFS_Mechanical_design
     The AO WFS camera lens must be provided with a 2 axis stage moving in the plane orthogonal to the optical axis.
The required performance are
- range: 
  Absorption cell: -> Absorption cell in the CU, Absorption cell in the FE
     In order to understand systematics affecting the wavelength solution, ANDES shall be equipped with an independent wavelength calibration probe (e.g. a
  Absorption cell in the CU: -> Absorption cell, CU_WP
     In order to understand systematics affecting the wavelength solution, the ANDES CU shall provide an independent wavelength calibration probe (e.g. abs
  Absorption cell in the FE: -> Absorption cell, FE_WP
     In order to understand systematics affecting the wavelength solution, the ANDES FE shall grant the volume and the interfaces to equip an independent w
  Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L1: -> [R-AND-85], [R-SYS-11].0a, [R-SYS-12].0a, [R-SYS-14].0a, [R-SYS-1].0b, [R-SYS-2].0b, [R-SYS-3].0a, [R-SYS-3].0d
     Test for wavelength accuracy on group L1 (FL+CU+UBV)
  Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L2: -> [R-AND-85], [R-SYS-11].0a, [R-SYS-12].0a, [R-SYS-14].0a, [R-SYS-1].0a, [R-SYS-2].0a, [R-SYS-3].0a, [R-SYS-3].0c
     Test for wavelength accuracy on group L2 (FE+FL+CU+RIZ)
  Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L3: -> [R-AND-85], [R-SYS-11].0a, [R-SYS-12].0a, [R-SYS-14].0a, [R-SYS-1].0c, [R-SYS-2].0c, [R-SYS-3].0a, [R-SYS-3].0b
     Test for wavelength accuracy on group L3 (FL+CU+YJH)
  Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L4: -> [R-AND-85]
     Test for wavelength accuracy on group L4 (FL+CU+K)
  [R-AND-84]: -> Acquisition Tests_2, Optical Architect, SW Architect - SWSE, Wavelength precision - L1, Wavelength precision - L2, Wavelength precision - L3, Wavelength precision - L4, [R-SYS-11].0a
     Wavelength precision (TLR-A.12 & TLR-A.20)
The Instrument shall achieve a precision in the wavelength calibration of better than 1 m/s RMS (goal: 0.1 
  [R-AND-85]: -> Acquisition Tests_3, Optical Architect, SW Architect - SWSE, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L1, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L2, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L3, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L4, [R-SYS-11].0a
     Stability of Wavelength Calibration Accuracy (TLR-A.12, TLR-A.17 & TLR-A.20)

The instrument shall achieve a short term (24 hours) stability of wavele
  [R-SYS-12].0a: -> CU_WP, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L1, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L2, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L3, Wavelength precision - L1, Wavelength precision - L2, Wavelength precision - L3, [R-AND-84]
     The Wavelength Calibration Source(s) and flatfield sources have to provide simultaneous coverage of the full ANDES wavelength range without gaps.
  [R-SYS-14].0a: -> CU_WP, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L1, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L2, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L3, Wavelength precision - L1, Wavelength precision - L2, Wavelength precision - L3, [R-AND-84]
     The Wavelength Calibration Source(s) shall be stable with a maximum relative error of 3E-10 (TBC) over 24h (goal: 6E-11 (TBC) over 10 years).
  [R-SYS-1].0a: -> RS_WP, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L2, Wavelength precision - L2, [R-AND-84], [R-AND-85]
     The average centroid of the spectral lines in the echellogram on the detector shall be stable better than 1 m/s over 24h (goal 10 cm/s).
  [R-SYS-1].0b: -> Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L1, US_WP, Wavelength precision - L1, [R-AND-84], [R-AND-85]
     The average centroid of the spectral lines in the echellogram on the detector shall be stable better than 1 m/s over 24h (goal 10 cm/s).
  [R-SYS-1].0c: -> Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L3, Wavelength precision - L3, YS_WP, [R-AND-84], [R-AND-85]
     The average centroid of the spectral lines in the echellogram on the detector shall be stable better than 1 m/s over 24h (goal 10 cm/s).
  [R-SYS-4].0a: -> SW Sys Eng, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L2, Stability of wavelength calibration accuracy - L3, Wavelength precision - L2, Wavelength precision - L3, [R-AND-84], [R-AND-85]
     The DRS shall provide a correction to the wavelength solution along the full slit by using spectra of the simultaneous reference acquired along with t

### Verification / satisfy links on the 14 WP requirements:
  [R-AND-65].3.SW2: none
  [R-AND-70].1.SW1: none
  [R-AND-112].1.SW1: none
  [R-AND-114].1.SW1: none
  [R-AND-115].1.SW1: none
  [R-AND-119].1.SW1: none
  [R-AND-120]: none
  [R-INS-896].1.SW1: none
  [R-INS-896].1.SW2: none
  [R-INS-896].1.SW3: none
  [R-INS-928]: none
  [R-INS-1049]: none
  [R-INS-1050]: none
  [R-AND-88].1.SW1: none

### 'Reduction Pipeline RV Error' element:
  RV Error RollUP Pattern (uml:Package)  path: RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  Total RV Error Budget (uml:Class)  path: RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  Total RV Error Budget (uml:StateMachine)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  Total RV Error Budget (uml:Diagram)  path: Total RV Error Budget < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  calibration RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  FE RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  spectrographs RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  FL RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  software Algorythms RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  FE RV Error (uml:Class)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  FE Guiding on fiber RV Error (uml:Property)  path: FE RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  FE Guiding on fiber RV Error (uml:Class)  path: FE RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  Calibration RV Error (uml:Class)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  CU Injecton RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Calibration RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  cal Source Stability RV Error (uml:Property)  path: Calibration RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  Cal Source Stability RV Error (uml:Class)  path: Calibration RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  Spectrographs RV Error (uml:Class)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  FL RV Error (uml:Class)  path: Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets < Model
  modal Noise RV Error (uml:Property)  path: FL RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  near-Field Scrambling RV Error (uml:Property)  path: FL RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  far-Field Scrambling RV Error (uml:Property)  path: FL RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  Modal Noise RV Error (uml:Class)  path: FL RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  Near-Field Scrambling RV Error (uml:Class)  path: FL RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets
  Far-Field Scrambling RV Error (uml:Class)  path: FL RV Error < Total RV Error Budget < RV Budget < Analysis & Budgets

Assistant2026-07-04T09:57:17.893Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-04T09:57:18.034Z

I dug a bit further in the model to ground this, and the answer to your last question is essentially no — and the model itself contains the evidence. Here's my assessment.

Is it a good flow-down, methodologically?

Yes, above average for what I see in practice. It's a real three-tier derivation (document requirement → SW-level → DRS-level) rather than a flat allocation; the leaves are quantified and verifiable (±5% resolution, 1% sky subtraction, 20% FWHM growth, 24 h calibration validity); source documents are kept as versioned baselines with stable IDs; and compliance status fields are maintained. The hygiene issues are minor but real: the [R-AND-88].1 → .SW1 link exists only by naming convention, the R-INS-896 branch uses containment while the TRS branch uses DeriveReqt (two mechanisms for the same semantics makes automated queries fragile), a couple of derivations are verbatim echoes of their parent ([R-AND-115].1 and .1.SW1 are identical — a derivation should add an architectural decision, not copy), and the texts could use a review pass ("degradate", "recored", "shall be fully compliance").

What's missing — and this is the interesting part

The radial-velocity thread never reaches the DRS work package. This is the flagship performance of the instrument, and the model knows it: the Analysis & Budgets / RV Budget package contains a Total RV Error Budget with an explicit "Reduction Pipeline RV Error" contributor under "Software Algorythms RV Error". But no requirement linked to SW DRS carries any RV or wavelength-accuracy allocation. Even more telling: [R-SYS-4].0a reads "The DRS shall provide a correction to the wavelength solution along the full slit by using spectra of the simultaneous reference…" — a DRS requirement by its own text — yet it's linked to the SW Sys Eng WP, not SW DRS. Similarly [R-AND-84] (wavelength precision better than 1 m/s RMS) and [R-AND-85] (calibration stability) flow to "SW Architect - SWSE" and the Optical Architect, then stop. The budget-to-requirement-to-WP thread exists in pieces but isn't closed.

Beyond RV, things I'd expect in a DRS spec that aren't among the 14:

  • Error propagation — per-pixel variance and quality flags on all products (a science-grade pipeline must-have; HDRL encourages but doesn't enforce it)
  • SNR / extraction efficiency — nothing bounds how much signal the reduction may lose relative to the photon limit
  • Flux/continuum fidelity — critical for transmission spectroscopy; only indirectly referenced via [R-AND-112]
  • Telluric correction — conspicuously absent for an instrument whose science lives in the red/NIR
  • Throughput/latency — no "reduce faster than acquisition" or QC-latency requirement, though [R-INS-896].1.SW1 implies night-time QC
  • Verification: none of the 14 has a Verify or Satisfy link. The model does do this elsewhere — the wavelength requirements link to "Wavelength precision – L1…L4" test cases — so the practice exists, it just hasn't been applied to the DRS set yet.

One currency flag: [R-INS-896].1.SW3 mandates Reflex, which ESO has been superseding with EDPS as the pipeline execution environment. That's inherited from the Common Requirements baseline and worth checking against ESO's current expectations.

Would a compliant DRS produce the expected results?

Fulfilling these 14 gives you an ESO-ecosystem-compliant pipeline (CPL/HDRL, Phase 3 products, DFS keywords) that doesn't smear the resolution, handles both modes, and subtracts sky to 1%. That's necessary but not sufficient. A contractor could satisfy every one of them and still deliver a DRS that misses the science, because nothing in this set binds the pipeline to the RV error budget, wavelength-solution accuracy, error propagation, or telluric handling. My guess is the real depth lives in the referenced E-AND-SW-SPE-* documents outside the model, so this is likely an incomplete linkage rather than an incomplete project — but as modeled, the flow-down is a compliance skeleton, not an acceptance basis. If I were doing the review, my top action items would be: allocate a quantified number from "Reduction Pipeline RV Error" into a DRS requirement, re-home [R-SYS-4].0a to the DRS WP, and add Verify links for the 14.

📝 Write drs-flowdown.md
/Users/tom/ANDES/cameo/drs-flowdown.md
# Requirements on the ANDES DRS work package — flow-down and assessment

Extracted from `ANDES_model.mdzip` (Cameo/MagicDraw XMI, no Cameo needed) on 2026-07-04.
Interactive diagram: `drs-flowdown.html` (also at https://claude.ai/code/artifact/59108c49-d09b-426a-bd3c-2eb304bf23f6).

## The 14 requirements linked to SW DRS

The work package is the `SW DRS` class in `6.WBS / ANDES_WP / SW Sys Eng`. Fourteen
requirements are linked to it via dependencies, from two source documents.

### ANDES Technical Requirements Specification (8)

Seven three-level derivation chains (document requirement -> SW-level -> DRS-level),
plus one requirement applying directly:

| Chain | DRS-level requirement (verbatim) |
|---|---|
| [R-AND-65] -> .3 -> **.3.SW2** | The ANDES DRS software shall be able to reduce data for seeing-limited mode data and IFU mode data |
| [R-AND-70] -> .1 -> **.1.SW1** | The DRS shall cover reduction procedure in the full wavelength range of ANDES and shall support the extension to U band and K band. The DRS shall be modular to tolerate gaps and further wavelength extensions. |
| [R-AND-88] -> .1 -> **.1.SW1** | The ANDES DRS shall not degradate the spectral resolution of the recored spectra more than [-5%; +5%] across the wavelength range |
| [R-AND-112] -> .1 -> **.1.SW1** | The ANDES DRS shall not require night-time calibrations to reduce the data and fullfil the requirements on accuracy, precisions, flux calibration and resolution. All day-time calibrations shall be valid for at least 24 hours. |
| [R-AND-114] -> .1 -> **.1.SW1** | For observations in which optimal sky subtraction is requested (one sub-FoV serving as simultaneous sky probe), the sky subtraction obtained by the DRS shall be better than 1% of the measured sky background between OH lines. |
| [R-AND-115] -> .1 -> **.1.SW1** | The ANDES Data Reduction, when interpolating onto a regular output grid, shall not increase the FWHM of a Nyquist sampled Gaussian by more than 20%. |
| [R-AND-119] -> .1 -> **.1.SW1** | DRS data product shall be fully compliance with the ELT data flow system as specified in AD-8 |
| **[R-AND-120]** (direct) | Data Reduction Software (DRS): the Pipeline shall be compliant with AD8; science grade data products for both seeing-limited and IFU modes. |

### Common Requirements for ELT Instruments (6)

[R-INS-896] -> [R-INS-896].1, which branches into three DRS-level requirements
(expressed by containment, not DeriveReqt), plus three applying directly:

- **[R-INS-896].1.SW1** — QC algorithms in the reduction cascade, QC parameters stored in FITS headers, processable during the night (RON, efficiency from standard stars, etc.)
- **[R-INS-896].1.SW2** — science grade reduction offline, interactively
- **[R-INS-896].1.SW3** — all DRS functionality based on CPL, HDRL and Reflex, supporting different use cases
- **[R-INS-928]** (direct) — reduced products conform to ESO DICD (DFS keywords) and the Science Data Product Standard for Phase 3
- **[R-INS-1049]** (direct) — science-grade reduction based on CPL and HDRL as provided by ESO (AD8 section 4.7)
- **[R-INS-1050]** (direct) — interactive data reduction adaptable to specific data sets, Phase 3-compliant products

## Assessment

### Methodology: above average

Real multi-level derivation rather than flat allocation; quantified, verifiable leaves
(±5% resolution, 1% sky subtraction, 20% FWHM growth, 24 h calibration validity);
source documents kept as versioned baselines with stable IDs; compliance status fields
maintained. Hygiene issues, minor but real:

- `[R-AND-88].1 -> [R-AND-88].1.SW1` exists only by naming convention; no modeled
  DeriveReqt relationship.
- The R-INS-896 branch expresses derivation by containment while the TRS branch uses
  DeriveReqt; two mechanisms for the same semantics makes automated queries fragile.
- Some derivations are verbatim echoes of their parent ([R-AND-115].1 and .1.SW1 are
  identical); a derivation should add an architectural decision, not copy.
- Texts need a review pass ("degradate", "recored", "fullfil", "shall be fully
  compliance").

### The main gap: the RV thread never reaches the DRS work package

The model itself knows the DRS is an RV error contributor: `Analysis & Budgets /
RV Budget / Total RV Error Budget` contains an explicit "Reduction Pipeline RV Error"
node under "Software Algorythms RV Error". But no requirement linked to SW DRS carries
any RV or wavelength-accuracy allocation. Concretely:

- **[R-SYS-4].0a** reads "The DRS shall provide a correction to the wavelength solution
  along the full slit by using spectra of the simultaneous reference..." — a DRS
  requirement by its own text — yet it is linked to the SW Sys Eng WP, not SW DRS.
- **[R-AND-84]** (wavelength precision better than 1 m/s RMS) and **[R-AND-85]**
  (stability of wavelength calibration accuracy) flow to "SW Architect - SWSE" and the
  Optical Architect, then stop.

### Other content gaps for a DRS specification

- Error propagation: no requirement for per-pixel variance and quality flags on products.
- SNR / extraction efficiency: nothing bounds signal loss relative to the photon limit.
- Flux/continuum fidelity: only indirectly referenced via [R-AND-112].
- Telluric correction: absent, despite the science living in the red/NIR.
- Throughput/latency: no "reduce faster than acquisition" or QC-latency requirement.
- Verification: none of the 14 has a Verify or Satisfy link. The practice exists
  elsewhere in the model (wavelength requirements link to "Wavelength precision -
  L1...L4" test cases) but has not been applied to the DRS set.
- Currency: [R-INS-896].1.SW3 mandates Reflex, which ESO has been superseding with
  EDPS; inherited from the Common Requirements baseline, worth checking.

### Verdict

Fulfilling these 14 yields an ESO-ecosystem-compliant pipeline (CPL/HDRL, Phase 3,
DFS keywords) that preserves resolution and sampling, handles both modes, and subtracts
sky to 1%. Necessary but not sufficient: nothing binds the pipeline to the RV error
budget, wavelength-solution accuracy, error propagation, or telluric handling — a fully
compliant DRS could still miss the science. The depth likely lives in the referenced
`E-AND-SW-SPE-*` documents outside the model, so this is probably incomplete linkage
rather than an incomplete project; but as modeled, the flow-down is a compliance
skeleton, not an acceptance basis.

Suggested actions:

1. Allocate a quantified number from "Reduction Pipeline RV Error" into a DRS-level
   requirement linked to the SW DRS WP.
2. Re-home [R-SYS-4].0a to the SW DRS WP.
3. Add the missing DeriveReqt for [R-AND-88].1 -> .1.SW1.
4. Add Verify links for the 14 (pattern already exists for the wavelength requirements).

## Method notes

Requirement-to-WP links are model dependencies whose client is the `SW DRS` class.
Upward chains follow DeriveReqt abstractions, requirement nesting (R-INS-896 subtree),
and in one case naming convention. Requirement texts are verbatim from the stereotype
attributes in the XMI (`sysml:Requirement`, `AstroMBSE_Profile:AstroMBSE_Requirement`,
`Local_Reqs_Profile:AstroMBSE_Requirement_Local`). Extraction scripts (`index_model.py`,
`trace_flowdown.py`, `gap_check.py`) are reusable for any other work package.

/Users/tom/.claude/projects/-Users-tom-ANDES-cameo/memory/andes-cameo-model-structure.md
---
name: andes-cameo-model-structure
description: How to parse the ANDES Cameo .mdzip files and the model's non-obvious stereotype/linkage conventions
metadata:
  type: project
---

The ANDES SysML model (`ANDES_model.mdzip`, ELT-HIRES/ANDES spectrograph) is a zip; the model
is standard XMI in `com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model` (~150 MB). `AstroMBSE_Profile.mdzip`
holds the custom profile. BINARY-* entries are RTF element descriptions, not diagrams; diagram
layouts are not recoverable without Cameo.

Non-obvious conventions (verified 2026-07-04):
- Requirement text lives in stereotype attributes `Id`/`Text` on several stereotypes:
  `sysml:Requirement`, `AstroMBSE_Profile:AstroMBSE_Requirement`,
  `AstroMBSE_Profile:AstroMBSE_Interface_Requirement`, and
  `Local_Reqs_Profile:AstroMBSE_Requirement_Local` (used for the working copies of the
  Common Requirements for ELT Instruments; the plain sysml one alone misses these).
- One model element can carry multiple dependency stereotypes (DeriveReqt + track).
- Requirement-to-work-package allocation = plain (unstereotyped) uml:Dependency with the
  WBS work-package class as client and the requirement as supplier.
- Flow-down is expressed three inconsistent ways: DeriveReqt abstractions (TRS chains),
  containment/nesting (R-INS-896 subtree), and naming convention only ([R-AND-88].1.SW1
  has no modeled link). Tracing tools must handle all three.
- Document section headings are also requirement-stereotyped classes; real requirements
  have bracketed names like [R-AND-65].

DRS work package analysis (2026-07-04): 14 reqs linked to `SW DRS` in 6.WBS; results and
SE assessment saved in `drs-flowdown.md` / `drs-flowdown.html` in /Users/tom/ANDES/cameo.
Key finding: RV/wavelength-accuracy thread never reaches the DRS WP ([R-SYS-4].0a is homed
to SW Sys Eng instead); no Verify/Satisfy links on any of the 14.

Assistant2026-07-04T10:08:27.432Z
📝 Write CLAUDE.md
/Users/tom/ANDES/cameo/CLAUDE.md
# ANDES Cameo model analysis

`ANDES_model.mdzip` and `AstroMBSE_Profile.mdzip` are Cameo Systems Modeler (MagicDraw)
projects for the ANDES spectrograph (ELT). They are zip archives; the model is standard
XMI (UML 2.5/SysML) and fully parseable without Cameo.

## File format

- The model is the zip entry `com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model` (~150 MB XML).
- `BINARY-*` entries are RTF element descriptions (embedded images), not diagrams.
- Diagram layouts are Cameo-proprietary; diagram names and contents are extractable,
  renderings are not.

## Model conventions (non-obvious, verified against the XMI)

- Requirement Id/Text live in stereotype attributes on: `sysml:Requirement`,
  `AstroMBSE_Profile:AstroMBSE_Requirement`, `AstroMBSE_Profile:AstroMBSE_Interface_Requirement`,
  `Local_Reqs_Profile:AstroMBSE_Requirement_Local` (working copies of the ESO Common
  Requirements use only the last one).
- Requirement-to-work-package allocation: plain unstereotyped `uml:Dependency`,
  WBS work-package class as client, requirement as supplier.
- Flow-down is expressed three ways: `DeriveReqt` abstractions, containment nesting
  (e.g. R-INS-896 subtree), and sometimes naming convention only (no modeled link).
- One dependency can carry multiple stereotypes (e.g. DeriveReqt + track).
- Document section headings are requirement-stereotyped classes too; real requirements
  have bracketed names like `[R-AND-65]`.

## Tools (in tools/, run with uv from this directory)

All have PEP 723 headers, so plain `uv run` works.

1. Extract: `unzip -o ANDES_model.mdzip -d extracted/` (extracted/ is disposable).
2. `uv run tools/outline.py extracted/com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model [depth]`
   — package tree and diagram list.
3. `uv run tools/index_model.py extracted/com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model model_index.pkl`
   — one-time index (elements, requirements, dependencies, stereotypes); the pickle is
   what the other scripts read. Takes a minute or two.
4. `uv run tools/trace_flowdown.py` — requirements linked to a WBS work package plus
   their upward derivation chains; writes `flowdown.json`. The WP element id is
   hardcoded near the top (currently SW DRS); find another WP's id via the index.
5. `uv run tools/gap_check.py` — the RV/wavelength-calibration gap analysis and
   Verify/Satisfy link check for the DRS requirement set; query patterns are reusable.
6. `uv run tools/gen_viz.py` — turns flowdown.json into the grid cells of
   drs-flowdown.html (row layout is hand-ordered for the DRS analysis).

## Results so far

- `drs-flowdown.md` — the 14 requirements on the SW DRS work package, derivation
  chains, and SE assessment (main finding: the RV error budget thread never reaches
  the DRS WP; no Verify/Satisfy links on any of the 14).
- `drs-flowdown.html` — self-contained flow-down diagram of the same.

/Users/tom/.claude/projects/-Users-tom-ANDES-cameo/memory/andes-cameo-model-structure.md
---
name: andes-cameo-model-structure
description: ANDES Cameo model analysis status; parsing details live in the project CLAUDE.md
metadata:
  type: project
---

The ANDES SysML model analysis setup (mdzip parsing, XMI conventions, tools/ scripts) is
documented in /Users/tom/ANDES/cameo/CLAUDE.md — read that first, don't rediscover it.

Status 2026-07-04: DRS work-package flow-down analysis done, results in drs-flowdown.md
and drs-flowdown.html there. Key SE finding Tom agreed with: the RV/wavelength-accuracy
thread never reaches the DRS WP ([R-SYS-4].0a homed to SW Sys Eng instead of SW DRS;
the "Reduction Pipeline RV Error" budget slot has no requirement allocation), and none
of the 14 DRS requirements has Verify/Satisfy links. Suggested model fixes are listed at
the end of drs-flowdown.md; Tom may want the same analysis for other work packages
(rerun tools/trace_flowdown.py with a different WP id).

Assistant2026-07-04T10:09:07.187Z

All set. The project directory now contains everything from this session:

  • drs-flowdown.html and drs-flowdown.md — the diagram and the assessment
  • tools/ — the five scripts: outline.py (package/diagram overview), index_model.py (builds the pickle index the others read), trace_flowdown.py (WP requirement tracing — WP id is hardcoded near the top, swap it for other work packages), gap_check.py (the RV-gap and Verify-link queries), gen_viz.py (diagram row generator)
  • CLAUDE.md — documents the mdzip format, the model's non-obvious conventions (the four requirement stereotypes, plain-dependency WP allocation, the three flow-down mechanisms), and the tool workflow from unzip through visualization

I verified the tools run via uv run from the project root — they carry PEP 723 headers so no project setup is needed. Note the extracted XMI and model_index.pkl live in the session scratchpad and aren't copied (150 MB+, regenerable in a couple of minutes with steps 1 and 3 in CLAUDE.md).

Tool reply2026-07-04T10:12:41.073Z

Assistant2026-07-04T10:12:51.612Z

Done — the repo is live at https://github.com/ivh/andes-drs-reqs.

What was set up:

  • Git repo initialized with main as the default branch, one root commit (5be5c48) containing the assessment (drs-flowdown.md), the diagram (drs-flowdown.html), the five tools/ scripts, and CLAUDE.md.
  • .gitignore excludes *.mdzip as requested, and I also added the regenerable intermediates the CLAUDE.md workflow produces (extracted/, model_index.pkl, flowdown.json, rows_fragment.html) so future runs don't dirty the repo — easy to trim if you'd rather track any of those.
  • Created as public under your ivh account and pushed; main is tracking origin/main.

One thing to be aware of since the repo is public: drs-flowdown.md and the HTML quote the requirement texts verbatim, including from ESO-published documents ([R-INS-], [R-AND-]). If any of that is consortium-internal, you may want to flip the repo to private (gh repo edit ivh/andes-drs-reqs --visibility private).

User2026-07-04T10:14:55.216Z

github does not render html files that are in th repo?

User2026-07-04T10:15:37.371Z

github does not render html files that are in th repo? or rather insists on showing the source instread of letting the browser render it

Assistant2026-07-04T10:15:54.915Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-04T10:15:56.327Z

Right — that's by design: GitHub's file viewer always shows source, and raw links are served as text/plain so browsers won't render them (an XSS precaution). The standard fix is GitHub Pages, which serves the same files as a real website. Let me enable it for the repo.

Assistant2026-07-04T10:15:57.514Z
$ Bash
Enable GitHub Pages on main branch root
gh api -X POST repos/ivh/andes-drs-reqs/pages -f "source[branch]=main" -f "source[path]=/" 2>&1

Assistant2026-07-04T10:16:53.196Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-04T10:16:53.235Z

Yes — that's intentional on GitHub's part: the repo file viewer always shows source, and raw.githubusercontent.com serves files as plain text specifically so browsers won't execute HTML from repos (an XSS protection). There's no setting to change that for repo files.

The standard solution is GitHub Pages, which I've just enabled for the repo (serving the main branch root). Once the first build finishes — typically a minute or two — the diagram will render at:

https://ivh.github.io/andes-drs-reqs/drs-flowdown.html

I've set a watch on the URL and will confirm as soon as it returns 200. Since Pages serves the branch tip, the page also auto-updates on every push, so it stays in sync with the repo — no separate publishing step needed.

Assistant2026-07-04T10:19:28.755Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-04T10:19:33.764Z
📝 Write README.md
/Users/tom/ANDES/cameo/README.md
# ANDES DRS requirements flow-down

Requirements applying to the Data Reduction Software (DRS) work package of the
ANDES spectrograph (ELT), extracted directly from the project's Cameo/MagicDraw
SysML model (XMI) without the Cameo software.

- [Flow-down diagram](https://ivh.github.io/andes-drs-reqs/drs-flowdown.html) —
  the 14 requirements linked to the SW DRS work package, traced to their source documents
- [Assessment](drs-flowdown.md) — the requirements verbatim, plus a systems-engineering
  critique of the flow-down and its gaps

`tools/` holds the extraction scripts; see `CLAUDE.md` for the model's XMI conventions
and how to rerun the analysis for other work packages. The `.mdzip` model files
themselves are not part of this repo.
Tool reply2026-07-04T10:19:33.786Z

Assistant2026-07-04T10:19:46.993Z

README added and pushed. It's intentionally brief: one sentence on what the repo is (DRS requirements extracted from the Cameo SysML model without the software), links to the rendered flow-down diagram on Pages and to drs-flowdown.md with the assessment, and a pointer to tools/ + CLAUDE.md for rerunning the analysis, with a note that the .mdzip files aren't included. It's live on the repo front page at https://github.com/ivh/andes-drs-reqs.
